title: Epic: Better-Beads platform engineering uplift
description:
## Outcome
Better-beads has machine-validatable schemas, structured metadata, telemetry hooks, eval harnesses, and a portability manifest — closing the platform engineering gaps identified in the 2026-06-07 assessment.

## Background
- Assessment scored the skill A+ on domain design, B- on platform engineering.
- Five work items: schemas (WI-1), metadata (WI-2), telemetry (WI-3a/3b), evals (WI-4), portability (WI-5).
- Plan path: `plans/better-beads-platform-engineering-uplift.md`.
- Execution defaults: stdlib-only, fail-closed, no producer shape changes, no user-content leakage.

## Closure contract
Do not close until every direct child is closed or explicitly closed as unnecessary with evidence.

## Children / intended order
1. UPLIFT.1 — schema inventory and route schema (unblocked).
2. UPLIFT.2 — gate/quality/triage schemas (after UPLIFT.1).
3. UPLIFT.3 — metadata dependency contract (unblocked, parallel with UPLIFT.1).
4. UPLIFT.4 — routing eval harness (after UPLIFT.1).
5. UPLIFT.5 — quality eval harness (after UPLIFT.2).
6. UPLIFT.6 — telemetry helper, schema, and docs (after UPLIFT.2 + UPLIFT.4 + UPLIFT.5).
7. UPLIFT.7 — route telemetry instrumentation (after UPLIFT.6).
8. UPLIFT.8 — quality-gate telemetry instrumentation (after UPLIFT.6).
9. UPLIFT.9 — gate-loop telemetry instrumentation (after UPLIFT.7 + UPLIFT.8).
10. UPLIFT.10 — portability manifest and notes (after UPLIFT.2 + UPLIFT.3).

## Scope / non-goals
- Do: schemas, metadata, telemetry, evals, portability for better-beads.
- Do not: change rubric, failure modes, reference library, gate architecture, or producer JSON shapes.
- Do not: create adapter layers for non-Beads trackers.

## Parent acceptance criteria
- All 10 child beads closed with validation evidence.
- `br dep cycles --json` reports no cycles.
- `scripts/test_schemas.sh` passes.
- `evals/run_evals.sh` passes.
- Existing tests pass: `test_bead_quality_gate.py`, `test_bead_gate_loop.sh`, `test_cli_robot_surfaces.sh`.

## Validation
```bash
br dep cycles --json
bv --robot-plan
bv --robot-insights
```

## Parallelization notes
- UPLIFT.1 and UPLIFT.3 can run in parallel (no file overlap).
- UPLIFT.7 and UPLIFT.8 can run in parallel after UPLIFT.6.
- All telemetry instrumentation (UPLIFT.7/8/9) is single-owner on the target script.
close_reason: Completed: all 10 direct child beads are closed. Validation passed: br dep cycles --json => count 0; bv --robot-plan and bv --robot-insights completed; bash skills/better-beads/scripts/test_schemas.sh passed; bash skills/better-beads/evals/run_evals.sh passed (14 routing cases plus quality eval); python3 skills/better-beads/scripts/test_bead_quality_gate.py passed (10 tests); bash skills/better-beads/scripts/test_bead_gate_loop.sh passed; bash skills/better-beads/scripts/test_cli_robot_surfaces.sh passed.

title: UPLIFT.1: Schema inventory and route schema
description:
## Outcome
`schemas/README.md` and `schemas/better-beads-route-v1.schema.json` exist. The route schema validates against current `bead_route.sh --json` output in multiple fixture modes. `scripts/test_schemas.sh` runs offline using only bash and python3 stdlib.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: Better-Beads platform engineering uplift epic.
- Plan: `plans/better-beads-platform-engineering-uplift.md` WI-1.

## Success criteria
- `schemas/README.md` lists the four covered contracts, their producer commands, deferred contracts, and a no-shape-change note.
- `schemas/better-beads-route-v1.schema.json` is valid JSON Schema (supported keyword subset only).
- `scripts/test_schemas.sh` creates temporary repos with fake `br`/`bv` shims and validates route output for cases: no-beads/no-plan, no-beads/weak-plan, graph-present/fake-br-list, graph-present/cycle-inspection-failed.
- Test output prints `ok schema=<file> case=<case> producer=<cmd>` per case.
- Schema uses strict top-level, tolerant nested (`additionalProperties: true` for tool-derived objects).
- Test uses only python3 stdlib and bash. No `jsonschema` package required.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do: route schema, schemas/README.md, test_schemas.sh scaffold with route cases.
- Do not: create schemas for dispatch-verdict, quality-gate, authoring-triage, or deferred surfaces.
- Do not: change any producer JSON output shape.
- Do not: add `schema`/`tool`/`version` fields to producers that lack them.

## Failure behavior
- Unsupported JSON Schema keyword in a schema file: `test_schemas.sh` must fail with the schema path and keyword name.
- Producer returns malformed JSON: test must fail closed with actionable stderr.
- Mode-specific top-level variance: document in README and use `anyOf`/optional fields.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- `scripts/bead_route.sh` — route JSON producer.
- `scripts/test_bead_gate_loop.sh` — existing fake `br`/`bv` pattern to reuse.
- `test/ROUTING-TRUTH-TABLE.md` — documents route contract cases.
- `references/QUALITY-GATES.md` — documents dispatch-verdict shape (deferred).

## Validation
```bash
bash skills/better-beads/scripts/test_schemas.sh route
bash skills/better-beads/scripts/test_schemas.sh
```

## Closure evidence
Close with: schema file path, README path, test_schemas.sh output showing all route cases pass, deferred contracts listed in README.
close_reason: Implemented schemas/README.md, better-beads-route-v1.schema.json, and offline test_schemas.sh route harness; required schema validations passed.

title: UPLIFT.10: Portability manifest and notes
description:
## Outcome
`manifest.json` and `PORTABILITY.md` exist. The manifest maps all advertised robot surfaces with schema coverage status. Tool dependencies reference `metadata.json` without duplication.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: Better-Beads platform engineering uplift epic.
- Plan: `plans/better-beads-platform-engineering-uplift.md` WI-5.

## Success criteria
- `manifest.json` contains: `name`, `version`, `description`, `author`, `triggers`, `exclusions`, `required_tools` (referencing metadata.json tool names), `surfaces` (per-surface: `command`, `description`, `mutates_repo`, `requires_tools`, `stdout_schema`, `stdout_schema_status`), `risk_level`, `resources`, `scripts`.
- Every surface from `better-beads capabilities --json`, `bead_route.sh capabilities --json`, `bead_gate_loop.sh capabilities --json`, and `bead_quality_gate.py capabilities --json` is present or explicitly deferred.
- `stdout_schema_status`: `covered` points to WI-1 schemas; uncovered surfaces marked `deferred`; markdown-only surfaces marked `markdown`.
- `required_tools` references tool names from `metadata.json`; does not duplicate version constraints.
- `PORTABILITY.md` documents what works without `br`/`bv`: rubric, failure modes, formatting guide, examples are portable; scripts and gates require the toolchain.
- `manifest.json` validates with `python3 -m json.tool`.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do: manifest.json, PORTABILITY.md, manifest validation.
- Do not: YAML format (use JSON for stdlib validation).
- Do not: adapter layers for non-Beads trackers.
- Do not: change any existing file except possibly schemas/README.md to cross-reference manifest.

## Failure behavior
- Manifest missing an advertised surface: validation must fail.
- Invalid JSON: must be fixed before merge.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- `skills/better-beads/metadata.json` — canonical dependency source.
- `skills/better-beads/schemas/README.md` — lists covered/deferred schemas.
- `skills/better-beads/SKILL.md` — current entrypoint.
- `skills/better-beads/references/README.md` — reading order.

## Validation
```bash
python3 -m json.tool skills/better-beads/manifest.json >/dev/null
# Validate surfaces against capabilities:
python3 -c "
import json, subprocess, sys
m = json.load(open('skills/better-beads/manifest.json'))
surface_names = {s['command'] for s in m['surfaces']}
print(f'Manifest surfaces: {len(surface_names)}')
print('manifest.json structure OK')
"
```

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Depends on: UPLIFT.2 (schemas to reference) and UPLIFT.3 (metadata to reference).

## Closure evidence
Close with: manifest.json path, PORTABILITY.md path, validation output, surface coverage summary.
close_reason: Completed UPLIFT.10: added skills/better-beads/manifest.json and skills/better-beads/PORTABILITY.md; manifest validates with python3 -m json.tool, required structure check passed, capability surface coverage check passed, and scripts/test_schemas.sh passed.

title: UPLIFT.3: Metadata dependency contract
description:
## Outcome
`metadata.json` declares required tools, test tools, compatibility, triggers, exclusions, and required capabilities with structured per-tool objects.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: Better-Beads platform engineering uplift epic.
- Plan: `plans/better-beads-platform-engineering-uplift.md` WI-2.

## Success criteria
- `required_tools` array with objects: `name`, `required_for`, `version_command`, `minimum_version` (or null), `version_detection`, `failure_behavior`.
- Required tools: `br`, `bv`, `python3` (≥3.10), `bash` (≥4.0).
- `test_tools` section: `scripts/test_schemas.sh` as required validator; `jsonschema` as optional.
- `compatibility.tested_with` records observed versions or `unknown`.
- `triggers`, `exclusions`, `required_capabilities` present.
- Existing fields preserved (`version`, `organization`, `date`, `abstract`, `references`).
- `metadata.json` is the canonical dependency source; no version duplication in manifest.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do: restructure metadata.json with the exact top-level sections from the plan.
- Do not: change SKILL.md, scripts, or reference docs.
- Do not: invent fake version pins for tools without version detection.

## Failure behavior
- Tools without reliable `--version`: record `version_detection: "not_supported"` and `minimum_version: null`.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- `skills/better-beads/metadata.json` — current minimal metadata.
- `skills/better-beads/SKILL.md` frontmatter — `name`, `description`.

## Validation
```bash
python3 -m json.tool skills/better-beads/metadata.json >/dev/null
python3 -c "
import json
d = json.load(open('skills/better-beads/metadata.json'))
assert 'required_tools' in d
assert 'test_tools' in d
assert 'compatibility' in d
for t in d['required_tools']:
    for k in ('name','required_for','version_command','minimum_version','version_detection','failure_behavior'):
        assert k in t, f'missing {k} in {t[\"name\"]}'
print('metadata.json structure OK')
"
```

## Closure evidence
Close with: metadata.json diff, validation script output, confirmation no other files changed.
close_reason: Completed metadata dependency contract in skills/better-beads/metadata.json. Validation passed: json.tool, required structure assertion, metadata dependency contract assertion, UBS JSON scan exit 0.

title: UPLIFT.4: Routing eval harness
description:
## Outcome
`evals/routing_eval.py` tests all 14 automatic routing cases (A1–A14) from the truth table using executable JSON case data and temporary repos with fake tools.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: Better-Beads platform engineering uplift epic.
- Plan: `plans/better-beads-platform-engineering-uplift.md` WI-4.

## Success criteria
- `evals/cases/routing_cases.json` defines all A1–A14 cases with expected `recommended_mode`, `graph_state`, `plan_readiness`, and `next_steps` substrings.
- `evals/routing_eval.py` creates temporary repos per case (no `.beads`, empty `.beads`, fixture `.beads/issues.jsonl`), fake `bin/br` shims prepended to PATH, optional plan files.
- Each case runs `bead_route.sh --repo TMP --json` and compares fields.
- `evals/run_evals.sh routing` exits 0 when all cases pass.
- `evals/README.md` documents how to run and update cases.
- No network access, no LLM calls, no dependency on caller's `.beads` graph.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do: routing eval, cases JSON, run_evals.sh scaffold, README.
- Do not: quality eval (that is UPLIFT.5).
- Do not: mutate any existing script or bead state.
- `test/golden/*.input.md` may remain as human reference; they are not the runtime input.

## Failure behavior
- Missing or broken `bead_route.sh`: eval reports tool error, does not silently pass.
- Case field mismatch: exit 1 with case name and expected-vs-actual diff.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- `skills/better-beads/scripts/bead_route.sh` — route tool under test.
- `skills/better-beads/test/ROUTING-TRUTH-TABLE.md` — human-readable case reference.
- `skills/better-beads/test/golden/` — existing golden fixture notes.
- `skills/better-beads/scripts/test_bead_gate_loop.sh` — fake tool pattern to reuse.

## Validation
```bash
bash skills/better-beads/evals/run_evals.sh routing
```

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Depends on: UPLIFT.1 (schemas enable optional schema validation in evals).
- Single-owner: `evals/` directory.

## Closure evidence
Close with: evals/run_evals.sh routing output showing 14 pass, routing_cases.json case count, evals/README.md path.
close_reason: Implemented routing eval harness under skills/better-beads/evals. Validation passed: run_evals.sh routing reports 14 routing cases passed; routing_cases.json count OK: 14; test_bead_route.sh passed; py_compile and json.tool passed; UBS changed eval files exited 0.

title: UPLIFT.5: Quality eval harness
description:
## Outcome
`evals/quality_eval.py` compares deterministic quality-gate output against a single canonical baseline file. Finding drift is detected without network or LLM calls.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: Better-Beads platform engineering uplift epic.
- Plan: `plans/better-beads-platform-engineering-uplift.md` WI-4.

## Success criteria
- `evals/quality_eval.py` reads `test/fixtures/example-graph.json`, runs `bead_quality_gate.py` with temp `.beads/issues.jsonl`, compares output against `evals/baselines/quality_gate_baseline.json`.
- Baseline fields: exit code, `issue_count`, `error_count`, `warning_count`, `operator_blocking_count`, `split_review_required_count`, sorted finding-code multiset, selected finding fields (`severity`, `issue_id`, `code`, `operator_blocking`, `requires_split_review`).
- `quality_eval.py --update-baseline` rewrites baseline with deterministic sort. Never runs by default from `run_evals.sh`.
- `evals/run_evals.sh quality` exits 0 when baseline matches.
- No score concepts — this is finding drift, not score drift.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do: quality eval, canonical baseline file, extend run_evals.sh and README.
- Do not: routing eval (UPLIFT.4), scoring rubric, LLM-based semantic eval.
- Do not: change quality-gate output or any existing scripts.

## Failure behavior
- Exit-code, error-count, operator-blocking-count, split-review-count, or finding-code multiset change: failure unless baseline explicitly updated.
- Warning-count-only drift: warning if verdict and finding-codes unchanged.
- Missing `bead_quality_gate.py`: fail with tool-error message.
- `--update-baseline` never auto-runs from `run_evals.sh`.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- `skills/better-beads/scripts/bead_quality_gate.py` — quality tool under test.
- `skills/better-beads/test/fixtures/example-graph.json` — fixture graph.
- `skills/better-beads/evals/run_evals.sh` — extended from UPLIFT.4.

## Validation
```bash
bash skills/better-beads/evals/run_evals.sh quality
bash skills/better-beads/evals/run_evals.sh
```

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Depends on: UPLIFT.2 (quality-gate schema available for optional validation).
- Single-owner: `evals/baselines/quality_gate_baseline.json`.

## Closure evidence
Close with: baseline file path, run_evals.sh output showing quality pass, evals/README.md updated.
close_reason: Implemented quality eval harness with deterministic baseline drift detection. Validation passed: run_evals.sh quality, run_evals.sh default routing+quality, baseline structure assertion, py_compile, json.tool, UBS changed eval files exited 0.

title: UPLIFT.6: Telemetry helper, schema, and docs
description:
## Outcome
A shared telemetry helper (`scripts/better_beads_telemetry.py`), telemetry JSON schema, and `references/TELEMETRY.md` exist. No existing script behavior changes.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: Better-Beads platform engineering uplift epic.
- Plan: `plans/better-beads-platform-engineering-uplift.md` WI-3a.

## Success criteria
- `scripts/better_beads_telemetry.py` can be imported by Python and invoked as CLI by shell.
- It owns: timestamp formatting (ISO 8601), JSON serialization, atomic append, telemetry failure handling.
- JSONL event fields: `run_id`, `timestamp`, `tool`, `tool_version`, `contract_version`, `mode`, `repo_id` (truncated SHA-256 of repo root, 16 hex), `repo_basename`, `duration_ms`, `exit_code`, `verdict`, `finding_counts`, `schema_version`.
- No absolute paths in telemetry events. No bead descriptions, plan text, prompts, or stdout.
- `schemas/better-beads-telemetry-v1.schema.json` validates helper output.
- `references/TELEMETRY.md` documents the contract.
- `scripts/test_schemas.sh telemetry` validates the schema.
- No existing script modified. This is contract-only.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do: helper, schema, docs, schema test extension.
- Do not: instrument `bead_route.sh`, `bead_quality_gate.py`, or `bead_gate_loop.sh` (that is UPLIFT.7/8/9).
- Do not: log user-authored content or absolute paths.

## Failure behavior
- Unwritable telemetry path: helper warns to stderr and returns success (caller decides exit code).
- Malformed append: single best-effort write; partial failure must not corrupt prior events.
- Helper self-test: `python3 scripts/better_beads_telemetry.py --self-test` exits 0.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- `scripts/bead_route.sh` — will consume helper (later bead).
- `scripts/bead_quality_gate.py` — will consume helper (later bead).
- `scripts/bead_gate_loop.sh` — will consume helper (later bead).
- `scripts/test_schemas.sh` — extend with telemetry schema case.

## Validation
```bash
python3 skills/better-beads/scripts/better_beads_telemetry.py --self-test
bash skills/better-beads/scripts/test_schemas.sh telemetry
```

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Depends on: UPLIFT.2 (schema infrastructure), UPLIFT.4 + UPLIFT.5 (evals exist before instrumentation).
- Blocks: UPLIFT.7, UPLIFT.8, UPLIFT.9.

## Closure evidence
Close with: helper path, schema path, TELEMETRY.md path, self-test output, test_schemas.sh telemetry output.
close_reason: Completed UPLIFT.6: added scripts/better_beads_telemetry.py, schemas/better-beads-telemetry-v1.schema.json, and references/TELEMETRY.md; extended test_schemas.sh telemetry; validation passed: helper self-test, telemetry schema target, full test_schemas.sh, import check, py_compile, json.tool, UBS clean, and br dep cycles.

title: UPLIFT.7: Route telemetry instrumentation
description:

title: Post-close continuation helper for Beads swarms
description:
## Outcome
A post-close helper prevents agents from stopping after one bead closes when that close unblocks more ready Beads work.

## Background
The skills NTM swarm falsely converged after agents closed assigned children and parked. Downstream work became ready, but no pane claimed it. Prompt-only marching orders are not durable at closeout time.

## Success criteria
- Helper lives under `skills/better-beads/scripts/` or the `scripts/better-beads` dispatcher.
- It closes one bead with evidence, runs `br sync --flush-only`, then checks ready/queue-dry state.
- JSON includes `closed_id`, `suggested_next_id`, `ready_count`, `queue_dry`, `must_continue`, and `suggested_command`.
- Ready work sets `must_continue=true` until the agent claims, assigns, or explicitly blocks the next item.
- No ready work distinguishes true queue-dry from tracker drift, stale JSONL, or remaining `in_progress` beads.

## Failure behavior
- Failed `br close` stops the helper before follow-up logic.
- Failed sync or tracker drift is reported; the helper must not claim queue-dry.
- Failed ready inspection is treated as unknown, not done.
- The helper never commits, pushes, deletes files, or mutates Beads internals directly.

## Known anchors
- `br close --suggest-next --json` is the close-plus-next primitive.
- `br sync --flush-only` exports JSONL after mutations.
- `br ready --json` is the canonical ready-work list.
- `skills/better-beads/scripts/bead_closeout_guard.sh` is the separate end-of-pass guard.
- `skills/better-beads/scripts/better-beads` is the dispatcher for public skill surfaces.
- `skills/better-beads/scripts/test_cli_robot_surfaces.sh` covers helper/dispatcher robot surfaces.

## Validation
Verify the implementation with fixture tests that close one bead and unblock another, plus a final-bead queue-dry fixture. The command `bash skills/better-beads/scripts/test_cli_robot_surfaces.sh` must pass if a dispatcher surface is added. The command `python3 skills/better-beads/scripts/bead_quality_gate.py --id skills-add-authoring-triage-json-entrypoint-565 --json` must report zero errors.

```bash
bash skills/better-beads/scripts/test_cli_robot_surfaces.sh
python3 skills/better-beads/scripts/bead_quality_gate.py --id skills-add-authoring-triage-json-entrypoint-565 --json
```

## Non-goals
- Do not make `br` run git operations.
- Do not install global shell hooks.
- Do not replace `bead_closeout_guard.sh`.

## Closure evidence
Close with the helper path, sample JSON for both fixture cases, validation output, and confirmation that no git side effects were added.

title: NTM watcher auto-dispatch for newly ready Beads work
description:
## Outcome
An NTM watcher prevents false convergence by detecting newly ready Beads work after closures and assigning idle panes with evidence.

## Background
During the skills swarm, all Codex panes parked even though UPLIFT.7 and UPLIFT.8 were ready. `ntm --robot-snapshot` reported `ready_work=0`, while queue-dry and Beads checks showed actionable work.

## Success criteria
- Watcher or operator recipe checks `ntm work queue-dry <session> --json`, `br ready --json`, and `ntm --robot-is-working=<session>`.
- Ready work plus idle panes produces targeted assignment via `ntm assign` or `ntm --robot-send`.
- Assignment targets specific idle panes and avoids generic broadcast nudges.
- Snapshot `ready_work=0` is non-authoritative when queue-dry reports tracker drift or ready IDs.
- Dependent work is not dispatched until dependency closure is visible in Beads.
- Evidence records ready IDs, idle panes, queue-dry reason, and any assignment sent.

## Failure behavior
- Failed queue-dry inspection blocks convergence claims.
- Stale Beads state or JSONL drift is reported instead of standing down.
- No idle panes means report ready work but send no prompt.
- Active reservation conflicts are reported instead of force-assigned.
- True queue-dry results in no synthetic work and no prompt spam.

## Known anchors
- `ntm work queue-dry <session> --json` exposes queue-dry, tracker drift, ready counts, and recommendations.
- `ntm --robot-is-working=<session>` exposes idle, working, and rate-limited panes.
- `ntm --robot-snapshot` is useful but not authoritative during tracker drift.
- `br ready --json` is the canonical ready-work list.
- `ntm assign <session> --auto --strategy=dependency` and `ntm --robot-send=<session> --panes=N --msg=...` are candidate actions.
- `vibing-with-ntm` convergence rules require queue-dry and no in-flight work.

## Validation
Verify with a ready-work case and a queue-dry case. In the ready-work case, run `ntm work queue-dry skills --json`, `br ready --json`, and `ntm --robot-is-working=skills`; the watcher must recommend or send one targeted assignment. In the queue-dry case, `ntm work queue-dry skills --json` must lead to stand-down/no-op. The command `python3 skills/better-beads/scripts/bead_quality_gate.py --id skills-add-authoring-triage-json-entrypoint-dhz --json` must report zero errors.

```bash
ntm work queue-dry skills --json
br ready --json
ntm --robot-is-working=skills
python3 skills/better-beads/scripts/bead_quality_gate.py --id skills-add-authoring-triage-json-entrypoint-dhz --json
```

## Non-goals
- Do not mutate `.beads` internals directly.
- Do not use agent self-report as convergence proof.
- Do not add broad periodic spam prompts.

## Closure evidence
Close with the watcher or recipe path, ready-work evidence, queue-dry no-op evidence, and the exact assignment command or recommendation emitted.

Validation passed: test_ntm_ready_watcher.sh; bash -n changed scripts; live ntm work queue-dry skills --json; br ready --json; ntm --robot-is-working=skills; ntm_ready_watcher.sh skills --json recommended targeted assignment when ready work and idle pane were visible; bead_quality_gate --id skills-add-authoring-triage-json-entrypoint-dhz --json reported 0 errors; UBS changed scripts exited 0. title: Add create-from-raw-plan quickpack
description:
## Outcome
Create-from-raw-plan mode has a compact quickpack so fresh agents can author a strong first graph without loading every long reference before they know what they need.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: Better-Beads authoring ergonomics feedback lane.
- Source feedback: fresh-plan mode required too much reference reading for the first usable pass.
- Preserves: the quickpack reduces navigation cost but does not lower the quality gates.

## Success criteria
- Add `CREATE-FROM-RAW-PLAN-QUICKPACK.md` or equivalent packaged reference.
- Quickpack includes one strong parent example, one strong child example, readiness gates, top failure modes, a compact formatting template, and post-mutation gate commands.
- `SKILL.md` and create-mode docs point fresh agents to the quickpack before deeper reference reading.
- Quickpack explicitly says when to fall back to full references and semantic review.
- Existing required references remain available and authoritative.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do: synthesize from existing Better-Beads references instead of inventing a separate quality bar.
- Do: keep the quickpack short enough to scan in one terminal view or two small panes.
- Do not: duplicate long examples wholesale.
- Do not: remove the required deep-reference workflow for serious graph creation or polishing.

## Failure behavior
- If the quickpack conflicts with the full references, update the quickpack to defer to the full reference.
- If the quickpack grows into another long manual, split examples into references and keep the quickpack compact.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- `skills/better-beads/SKILL.md`.
- `skills/better-beads/references/MODE-CREATE-FROM-RAW-PLAN.md`.
- `skills/better-beads/references/GOOD-BEAD-EXAMPLES.md`.
- `skills/better-beads/references/FAILURE-MODES.md`.
- `skills/better-beads/references/BEAD-FORMATTING.md`.

## Validation
```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
quick = Path('skills/better-beads/references/CREATE-FROM-RAW-PLAN-QUICKPACK.md')
text = quick.read_text()
for needle in ['parent', 'child', 'readiness', 'failure', 'Validation', 'gate']:
    assert needle.lower() in text.lower(), needle
assert len(text.splitlines()) <= 220
PY
```
Expected: quickpack exists, covers required authoring aids, and stays compact.

## Dependency / parallel notes

title: Add plan-readiness routing and weak-plan detection
description:
## Outcome
The router can directly recommend `improve-plan-first` when a supplied plan is structurally weak, while create routes expose explicit plan-readiness gate metadata.

## Parent / source of truth

- Create-route branches include `plan_readiness.check_required: true`, gate reference, required gate names, and `alternate_mode_if_weak: "improve-plan-first"`.
- `bead_route.sh --plan PATH --json` performs a shallow structural check over all seven readiness gates.
- Weak plans route to `improve-plan-first` with `plan_readiness.status: "weak"` and `missing_gates` populated.
- Structurally ready plans preserve the graph-state route and report `status: "structurally_ready"` with no missing gates.

- Do not: pretend the shallow check is semantic quality review; it only checks section/term presence.
- Do not: mutate bead state from `--plan`.

## Failure behavior
- Missing or unreadable `--plan` path exits 2 with a clear stderr diagnostic.
- Weak plan output must identify missing gates instead of creating beads or suppressing the issue.

## Known anchors / surfaces

title: Add safe graph draft/apply helper
description:
## Outcome
Agents can create a reviewed bead graph from a structured draft without writing ad hoc scripts or manually repairing dependency mistakes.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: Better-Beads authoring ergonomics feedback lane.
- Source feedback: previous graph creation required a long one-off Python script and manual dependency repair.
- Preserves: raw-plan mode may auto-create beads only after readiness and gates pass.

## Success criteria
- Add a local helper such as `better-beads create-graph --dry-run graph.json` and `--apply graph.json`, or a clearly named equivalent.
- Draft schema supports issues, descriptions, labels, priorities, parent closure relationships, blocking dependencies, and ready-frontier labels.
- Dry-run previews issue IDs/titles, dependencies, parent closure shape, possible cycles, blocked ready labels, and files/commands it will run.
- Apply creates beads with `br`, adds dependencies in a deterministic order, and fails closed before partial mutation when preflight detects invalid references.
- Tests cover dry-run-only behavior, unknown dependency references, parent closure dependencies, implementation order dependencies, and generated JSON parseability.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do: build a safe helper over `br` for Better-Beads authoring.
- Do: document the graph draft schema with a minimal example.
- Do not: bypass `br` audit/sync behavior.
- Do not: make LLM-generated graph drafts trusted without dry-run review and gates.

## Failure behavior
- Invalid draft JSON exits nonzero with a field/path-specific error.
- Unknown dependency or duplicate slug/title fails before mutation.
- If `br` fails during apply, the helper reports the last successful mutation and the exact recovery command/evidence needed.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- Dispatcher: `skills/better-beads/scripts/better-beads`.
- Existing scripts style: `skills/better-beads/scripts/bead_route.sh`, `bead_gate_loop.sh`, and Python gate helper.
- Tests: `skills/better-beads/scripts/test_cli_robot_surfaces.sh` plus a new focused helper test if needed.
- Docs: parent/dependency cookbook and create-mode quickpack.

## Validation
```bash
skills/better-beads/scripts/better-beads create-graph --dry-run skills/better-beads/test/fixtures/example-graph.json | python3 -m json.tool >/dev/null
bash skills/better-beads/scripts/test_cli_robot_surfaces.sh
```
Expected: dry-run is read-only, apply is explicit, and invalid drafts fail before mutation.

## Dependency / parallel notes
- Depends on the parent/dependency cookbook so helper semantics match the documented safe graph recipe.
- Single-owner risk: dispatcher and graph mutation behavior.

## Closure evidence
Close with: schema doc, dry-run/apply tests, validation results, and recovery behavior notes.

title: Improve Better-Beads authoring ergonomics from usage feedback
description:
## Outcome
Better-Beads becomes easier for fresh agents to operate after route selection.

## Background
- Source feedback: attached agent usage feedback from building the current Better-Beads routing/evals graph.
- Existing coverage: the current routing/evals lane already covers weak-plan detection, `--plan PATH` routing, route schema docs, and improve-plan inspection docs.
- Remaining feedback:
  - parent/dependency confusion,
  - heavy reference load,
  - ad hoc graph creation scripts,
  - missing frontier view,
  - prose-first gate output,
  - semantic-review glue,
  - `bv --robot-*` worktree side effects.

## Closure contract
Do not close this parent until every child below is closed or explicitly closed as unnecessary with evidence.

## Children / intended order
1. `skills-publish-br-graph-construction-cookbook-nf7` — document current graph construction semantics.
2. `skills-add-create-from-raw-plan-quickpack-yx1` — reduce fresh-plan reference load.
3. `skills-add-authoring-triage-json-entrypoint-8gi` — expose one first-command authoring surface.
4. `skills-add-safe-graph-draft-apply-helper-d6a` — make graph mutation dry-runnable and structured.
5. `skills-add-frontier-json-ready-label-truth-wzq` — show ready-label truth directly.
6. `skills-stable-json-operator-gate-envelopes-iik` — make operator gates machine-readable.
7. `skills-add-semantic-pack-helper-4v9` — package semantic-review artifacts.
8. `skills-fence-read-only-robot-surfaces-zkc` — fence read-side workspace mutation.

## Scope
- Improve the Better-Beads skill package and local scripts/docs.
- Keep docs portable for `npx skills add benvenker/skills --skill better-beads`.
- Preserve route-first workflow and the current high quality bar.
- Prefer deterministic local helpers over ad hoc shell glue.

## Non-goals
- Do not reimplement Beads itself unless the skill owns a wrapper/helper.
- Do not create GitHub issues or external tracker state.
- Do not weaken semantic review; make it easier to collect and run.
- Do not fold this lane into the existing routing/evals parent unless implementation proves a route-owned field is required.

## Parent acceptance criteria
- All direct child beads are closed or explicitly closed as unnecessary with evidence.
- Fresh agents can move from route to authoring/mutation/gating with documented commands.
- Read-side Better-Beads commands preserve the worktree or clearly isolate unavoidable side effects.
- Operator dispatch artifacts have stable machine-readable entrypoints.
- Final validation passes or has explicit follow-up beads for intentional deferrals.

## Validation
```bash
br dep cycles --json
bv --robot-plan
bv --robot-insights
bash skills/better-beads/scripts/test_cli_robot_surfaces.sh
python3 skills/better-beads/scripts/test_bead_quality_gate.py
bash skills/better-beads/scripts/test_bead_gate_loop.sh
skills/better-beads/scripts/bead_closeout_guard.sh
```
Expected: commands pass, or child close evidence explains intentionally deferred validation.

## Parallelization notes
- Docs-only cookbook/quickpack work can run before most helper code.
- Dispatcher command additions are single-owner work on `skills/better-beads/scripts/better-beads`.

title: Fence read-only robot surfaces against workspace mutation
description:
## Outcome
Better-Beads read/robot inspection surfaces stop surprising agents with workspace mutations.

## Parent / source of truth
- Parent: Better-Beads authoring ergonomics feedback lane.

- Preserves: `br --json` and `bv --robot-*` remain the recommended inspection surfaces, but read-side surprises must be fenced.

## Success criteria
- A regression check detects whether recommended Better-Beads inspection commands modify tracked files such as `.gitignore`, skill docs, or `.beads/issues.jsonl` unexpectedly.
- If the side effect can be avoided locally, Better-Beads wrappers/docs use the no-write mode or isolated environment.
- If the side effect cannot be avoided locally, docs warn clearly and provide a cleanup/isolation recommendation for agents before dispatch.
- `authoring-triage`, `frontier`, and `semantic-pack` read modes inherit the same no-mutation discipline.
- Robot-surface tests fail if a read-only command dirties the worktree without an explicit allowed artifact.

## Scope / non-goals
- Do: update Better-Beads helpers/docs/tests to fence read-side mutation.
- Do: prefer fixing or wrapping local command invocation when possible.
- Do not: patch external `bv` source unless that source is vendored and owned here.
- Do not: hide legitimate `.beads` mutations from explicit `br create/update/dep` commands.

## Failure behavior
- If a read-only command dirties tracked files, the test reports the exact files and command.
- If an unavoidable external side effect remains, the docs must identify it and the recommended cleanup path.

## Known anchors / surfaces
- Inspection docs: `skills/better-beads/SKILL.md`, `references/AUTHORING-PROMPTS.md`, `QUALITY-GATES.md`, `SEMANTIC-GATE.md`.

title: Harden route inspection and status decisions with fixtures
description:

- Malformed `br list` JSON -> exit 2 with no misleading route JSON.
- A `br list` JSON error envelope -> exit 2 with no misleading route JSON.
- `br dep cycles --json` failure does not silently become zero cycles; route JSON reports `cycle_inspection: "failed"`, `cycle_count: null`, and populated `inspection_warnings`.

## Failure behavior

- Unsafe bead inspection must not create stdout JSON that robot callers could treat as a valid route.

- malformed, nonzero, and error-envelope `br list`;
